The joy you exude on this is brilliant.

author: @totherarf
text:
How to make a smoothie.

Step 1. .... Hire a Luke. 

On a serious note, it is a way to make you realise how much energy we chuck at stuff nowadays!


On a really serious note ...... Chuck some Icecream in there you heathens! It is the only acceptable way of a man eating raw fruit
...... except for Pineapple!

author: @aadhithvarathan5432
text:
Hello Sir, 
I am an ardent fan of your videos. Very happy to see a scientist who does R&D on self-sufficiency putting videos on YouTube. We are both similar minded individuals. You have taught and motivated me to create my first Aluminium AA battery at home that ran successfully. Hope to see you one day after this pandemic finishes. Lets revolutionize the world.

author: @MisterRorschach90
text: This would make for an interesting gym/smoothie bar. You come in and build the smoothie but can only drink it after you’ve done a certain amount of time on the bike. You could set up gears that let you change the amount of time it takes to fully blend. Say someone wants to bike for 20 minutes, just find the gear ratio that fully blends a smoothie in 20 minutes. Someone in a hurry? Do it direct without gears or gears that make it faster.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: interesting plan mate

author: @Buzzhumma
text: Very smooth mate! I was thinking you could use a bikes gearing to change the rpm if needed!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I like that mate and think it would work well

replies.author: @AdricM
replies.text: hes already got some speed control in the belt pully system he had in the last vid.  but yeah if you were building this without the gear box hes using,   id probably suggest 2 or so sets of chain reductions with the final one being changeable with a rear hub sprocket.  and possibly a multig gear pedal chain wheel.  to modify the gearing on that end as well.  its easy to over think it.  hes currently got a fixed chain, to a fixed gear box,  to a belt/pully  to a right angle.  there are lots of places for loss,  but thats where its a prototype next one maybe it goes direct to a flywheel and then out a clutch to the output via a gear box or torque converter and some sort of flex shaft in version 3.0

author: @OKFrax-ys2op

author: @DimitriPappas
text: Every smoothie maker should be sold like this, that way we're reminded that we need to "work" for a smoothie, not just press a button - lol 😂 Would be cool to see how effective it is at crushing ice (or something stubborn like semi-frozen peanut butter with some added honey), ie how much more work is required on the pedaling side and whether the mod would still hold up under such load ;) Definitely a good motivator (reward) to get one's workout in for the day 😆 PS always great to see the two of you together in a video, joking about and having some genuine father-son fun! :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I plan on trying it out over some hard to do materials for sure - cheers mate
